steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Identify angles and use 30-60-90 triangle ratios
steps.content:
Angle B is 90° (right angle) and angle C is 30°, so angle A = 180° − 90° − 30° = 60°. This is a 30-60-90 special right triangle. Since the hypotenuse is 124:
$$AB = 124 \cdot \frac{1}{2} = 62$$
$$BC = 124 \cdot \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} = 62\sqrt{3}$$
Here, AB is opposite the 30° angle and BC is opposite the 60° angle.

steps.title: Calculate tan A
steps.content:
From angle A, the opposite side is $BC = 62\sqrt{3}$ and the adjacent side is $AB = 62$. Therefore:
$$\tan A = \frac{62\sqrt{3}}{62} = \sqrt{3} \approx 1.732$$
